page: Microsoft Azure D4d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled with a 154 GB local disk, and there are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside weaker multi-core and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench single-core score of 1566.0 rank in the average tier. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 3357.0 and the stress-ng multi-core metric of 3867.66, fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth tests show restricted throughput, with uncached large-block read speeds at 29.03 GB/sec and write speeds at 24.04 GB/sec, both placing in the bottom quartile.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI, which aid in cryptography and vector calculations. This is reflected in the average-tier Passmark CPU encryption test result of 3624.53. Conversely, database operations and web serving throughput are relatively low, with Redis SET operations registering at 3.88 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 6.17 GB/sec. Small-scale language model inference is possible, with text generation for a 135M model reaching an average speed of 107.36 tokens per second, though larger models like the 7B variant perform poorly at 4.53 tokens per second.

Given its resource density and moderate single-thread performance, the D4d_v5 offers a standard cost-efficiency profile for general-purpose workloads that do not demand high memory bandwidth or intensive parallel processing. It is suitable for light web serving, basic text processing, single-threaded applications, and development environments. It is less suited for high-throughput databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or complex mathematical modeling.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores), 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 154 GB local disk, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show a clear division: single-core CPU execution and text processing achieve average results, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth fall into the weaker tier. The instance supports AVX-512 instructions, assisting in basic cryptographic tasks, but exhibits lower throughput in database operations and web serving. This virtual machine is best suited for general-purpose workloads, light web hosting, and development environments that require a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio without demanding high-performance multi-threaded computing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and a 154 GB local disk, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor on the Hyper-V hypervisor.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4d_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, running on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, integrated local storage, and standard single-threaded performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Average single-core CPU and text processing performance
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for cryptographic workloads
Best suited for light web serving, development, and general-purpose tasks
